intro: Rhamnus seravschanica is a species of flowering plant in the family Rhamnaceae, commonly known as a buckthorn or a frangula. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its potential to aid in the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues, such as constipation and diarrhea, due to its laxative properties. The bioactive constituents of Rhamnus seravschanica include anthraquinones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Rhamnus seravschanica is often used to make herbal preparations such as tea, capsules, and extracts, which are used to support digestive health and promote bowel regularity.
